Jesus Jaime Valle, Concepcion Valle Hernandez, And Alexis Enrique Trinidad Moreno, Cross-Defendants, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Third Amended Cross-Complaint Of Maria Garcia
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTs JESUS VALLE, ALEXIS TRINIDAD MORENO, and CONCEPCION HERNANDEZ's Motion To Strike Portions Of The Third Amended Cross-Complaint Of Maria Garcia:

Cross-defendants' motion to strike sham allegations is denied. As explained in the ruling on the demurrer, the sham pleading doctrine does not apply. The motion to strike is denied as to the claim for punitive damages. The allegations in tort causes of action (claims 2, 3, 5, 9, 12, and 13) support the claim.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTs JESUS VALLE, ALEXIS TRINIDAD MORENO, and CONCEPCION HERNANDEZ's DEMURRER to 3RD Amended CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-defendants' demurrer to the third amended cross-complaint (TACC) is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone." Counsel's June 19, 2020 declaration concedes that has not occurred as to the TACC. The parties are ordered to immediately meet and confer. Cross-defendants' response to the TACC is due by August 24, 2020.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File A First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Terence O. Mayo, Esq., of MAYO & MAYO, T:415.397.1515, for defendant/moving party;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, Esq. of VOLKOV LAW OFFICE, T:415.987.7000, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MARIA GARCIA's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Cross-Complaint, as follows:

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Maria De Jesus Garcia's motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted on the condition that Defendant shall make herself available within 30 days for a renewed deposition as to the newly-asserted allegations. To the extent that plaintiff seeks to raise challenges to the proposed cross-complaint, he may file a demurrer or other appropriate motion.

The Court denied plaintiff's counsel's request for a ruling on plaintiff's objection to evidence.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
